info.title: wan2.7-image-to-video API
info.description: Generate videos from images using the WAN2.7 model, supporting first/last frame images, reference video clips, and audio-driven generation

post.description:
- WAN2.7 (wan2.7-image-to-video) model supports image-to-video generation
- Supports the following 6 material combinations, invalid combinations will return an error:
  1. First frame image (`image_start`)
  2. First frame image + audio (`image_start` + `audio_url`)
  3. First frame image + last frame image (`image_start` + `image_end`)
  4. First frame image + last frame image + audio (`image_start` + `image_end` + `audio_url`)
  5. Video continuation (`video_urls`)
  6. Video continuation + last frame image (`video_urls` + `image_end`)
- Note: `image_start` and `video_urls` are mutually exclusive; `video_urls` cannot be used with `audio_url` simultaneously
- Asynchronous processing mode, use the returned task ID to [query status](/en/api-manual/task-management/get-task-detail)
- Generated video links are valid for 24 hours, please save them promptly

error.message: Insufficient quota. Please top up your account.
Token does not have access to model: wan2.7-image-to-video
Too many requests, please try again later

prompt.description: Text prompt describing the video content to generate, limited to 5000 characters

negative_prompt.description: Negative prompt describing content to avoid in the video, limited to 500 characters

image_start.description:
First frame image URL (first_frame), mutually exclusive with video_urls

**Image Restrictions:**
- Formats: JPEG, JPG, PNG (transparent channels not supported), BMP, WEBP
- Resolution: Width and height range `[240, 8000]` pixels
- Aspect ratio: 1:8 to 8:1
- File size: Up to `20MB`

image_end.description:
Last frame image URL (last_frame), can be used with image_start or video_urls, cannot be used alone

**Image Restrictions:**
- Formats: JPEG, JPG, PNG (transparent channels not supported), BMP, WEBP
- Resolution: Width and height range `[240, 8000]` pixels
- Aspect ratio: 1:8 to 8:1
- File size: Up to `20MB`

video_urls.description:
Video continuation URL array (first_clip), only video_urls[0] is used. Mutually exclusive with image_start, cannot be used with audio_url simultaneously

**Video Restrictions:**
- Formats: mp4, mov
- Duration: `2~10` seconds
- Resolution: Width and height range `[240, 4096]` pixels
- Aspect ratio: 1:8 to 8:1
- File size: Up to `100MB`

**Continuation Details:**
- The model will continue generating based on the video content, with the continuation duration upper limit controlled by the duration parameter
- For example: when duration=15, if the input video is 3 seconds, the model will generate 12 additional seconds, resulting in a total output video of 15 seconds, billed at 15 seconds

audio_url.description:
Audio file URL (driving_audio), only available in first frame image mode, cannot be used with video_urls simultaneously

**Format Requirements:**
- Supported formats: `wav`, `mp3`
- Duration range: `2~30` seconds
- File size: Up to `15MB`

**Overflow Handling:**
- If the audio length exceeds the `duration` value, the first N seconds will be automatically extracted, and the rest will be discarded
- If the audio length is shorter than the video duration, the portion exceeding the audio length will be silent. For example, if the audio is 3 seconds and the video duration is 5 seconds, the output video will have sound for the first 3 seconds and be silent for the last 2 seconds

quality.description:
Video quality, defaults to `720p`

**Options:**
- `720p`: Standard definition, standard price, this is the default
- `1080p`: High definition, higher price

duration.description:
Video duration (seconds), range 2-15

**Note:**
- Supports any integer value between `2~15` seconds
- Each request will be pre-charged based on the `duration` value, actual charge is based on the generated video duration

seed.description: Random seed for reproducible generation results

prompt_extend.description: Whether to enable intelligent prompt rewriting. When enabled, a large model will optimize the prompt, which significantly improves results for simple or insufficiently descriptive prompts. Default is `true`

callback_url.description:
HTTPS callback URL for task completion

**Callback Timing:**
- Triggered when task is completed, failed, or cancelled
- Sent after billing confirmation

**Security Restrictions:**
- Only HTTPS protocol is supported
- Callbacks to internal IP addresses are prohibited (127.0.0.1, 10.x.x.x, 172.16-31.x.x, 192.168.x.x, etc.)
- URL length must not exceed `2048` characters

**Callback Mechanism:**
- Timeout: `10` seconds
- Up to `3` retries after failure (retries at `1`/`2`/`4` seconds after failure)
- Callback response format is consistent with the task query API response
- 2xx status codes are considered successful, other status codes trigger retries

can_cancel.description: Whether the task can be cancelled

bearerAuth.description:
## All APIs require Bearer Token authentication ##

**Get API Key:**

Visit [API Key Management Page](https://evolink.ai/dashboard/keys) to get your API Key

**Add to request header:**
```
Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY
```
